description_long: This object is a solar energy infrastructure installation consisting of a rectangular array of photovoltaic panels mounted on a single vertical pole. The solar panel array is composed of multiple dark blue-black solar cells arranged in a grid, framed by a metallic border, and supported by a robust metal structure. The pole is anchored into a concrete base, which sits on a circular patch of grass, providing stability and resistance to environmental forces. The back of the panel reveals a grid-like support structure, likely made of metal, which ensures rigidity and proper orientation. The overall design is minimalistic and functional, with no visible cabling or inverter components. This type of installation is commonly found in parks, sidewalks, or open urban spaces to harness solar energy for public amenities.
description: A pole-mounted solar panel array with a concrete base, featuring a grid of dark photovoltaic cells and a sturdy metal support structure.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full face of the solar panel array with visible photovoltaic cells and the supporting pole anchored in concrete.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the tilt of the solar panel and the thickness of the array, with the supporting pole and base visible.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the grid-like metal support structure behind the solar panels and the vertical pole.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the angled profile of the solar panel and the mounting details on the pole.
attribute_panel_orientation: tilted at approximately 30 degrees, facing forward (relative to view 0), orientation appears fixed
attribute_mounting_structure_type: pole-mounted with a concrete base and metal support frame
attribute_array_configuration: 2x3 grid of solar panels in a single rectangular array
